pages.intro: Choose the schedule that fits your teaching or learning rhythm. These pacing plans expand on the 3 pacing options introduced in the How to Use page — pick the one that fits and adapt as needed.

sections.title: 1-Week Intensive Plan
sections.summary: For summer camps, spring break, or focused CS immersion. 2–3 activities per day for 5–7 days.

items.text: What's the difference between random steps and an algorithm?
Do the Loop Bonus outside (pattern hunt)
Draw a decision tree for your morning routine
Do the "Sort Real Things" bonus with household objects
How is selection sort different from bubble sort?
Take a break — this one is meaty!
Complete the Vocabulary Review, Mini-Quiz, and Reflection Page
Celebration! Kids get to teach someone one concept

sections.title: 4-Week Unit Plan
sections.summary: For homeschool, after-school programs, or supplementary CS. 3 activities per week for 4 weeks.

items.text: Weekly reflection: Where did you see loops today?
Design a decision tree for a weekly household task
Do the "Algorithm Race" bonus with a family member
Complete Vocabulary Review, Mini-Quiz, and Reflection Page

sections.title: 12-Week Semester Plan
sections.summary: For elementary CS class or gentle weekly rhythm. 1 activity per week for 12 weeks.

pages.closing_reminder: Reminder: Every activity has both a Basic (ages 8-10) and Challenge (ages 10-12) version. Kids can revisit any activity in Challenge mode after completing Basic — that's the built-in extension.

pages.intro: Answers for every Basic and Challenge exercise. Bonus activities are open-ended and don't have single correct answers — see grading notes.

basic.label: Write 5 Sandwich Steps
basic.content: Open-ended. Grade against the Self-Check criteria: at least 5 steps, steps in a workable order, steps are specific (e.g., "open the bread bag," not "get bread").

challenge.label: Test Your Algorithm on Someone
challenge.content: Open-ended. The goal is that the tester follows the steps literally and the kid identifies what was missing or ambiguous. Any honest gap they spot (didn't say to open the jar, didn't mention a knife) counts as success.

basic.label: Number the 8 morning steps
basic.note: (Accept reasonable variations: wash face / brush teeth may swap; eat breakfast may come before get dressed. "Wake up" must be first; "Go to school" must be last.)

challenge.label: Spot the Broken Sequence
items: **Algorithm A (Making Cereal):** Step 3 "Eat the cereal" is in the wrong place — it should be LAST, after getting the spoon.
**Algorithm B (Soccer Practice):** Step 1 "Put on your soccer cleats" is in the wrong place — cleats go on AFTER socks, so it belongs after step 2.

bonus.label: Brushing Your Teeth correct order:
items: Wet the toothbrush under the tap
Spit out the toothpaste and rinse your mouth
Rinse the toothbrush and put it back
bonus.note: (Accept wetting the brush before or after applying toothpaste.)

basic.label: Find the Repeat
items: **Song:** "If you're happy and you know it, clap your hands (clap clap)" repeats — 3 times.
**P.E. Warm-Up:** "Do 10 jumping jacks" repeats 3 times (with "rest 5 seconds" repeating twice in between).
**Pancakes:** The whole pour → wait → flip → cook → remove cycle repeats once per pancake — 5 times for 5 pancakes.
**Brushing Teeth:** Brushing repeats twice per day (morning + night) — 14 times in a week.

challenge.label: Loop Detective
items: **Scenario A:** Repeat "call the next name and hear 'Here!'" 25 times.
**Scenario B:** Repeat "set one place (plate, fork, knife, spoon, napkin)" 4 times.

bonus.label: Spot the Loop in a Pattern
items: **Pattern A:** Triangle, circle, square repeats. Next 3 shapes: triangle, circle, square.
**Pattern B:** The loop is 4 moves: Step Left → Clap → Step Right → Clap.
**Pattern C:** Trash / Recycling alternates. Loop length: 2 days.

activities.title: Write Your Own Loop

basic.label: Loop It!
items: Scenario 4: **REPEAT "put one sticker on a card" 12 TIMES**
**Part A A1:** REPEAT "pour water into glass" **UNTIL** glass is full
**Part A A2:** REPEAT "practice free throw" **UNTIL** 10 in a row are made
**Part B:** REPEAT 4 TIMES { REPEAT "hand quiz to next student" 5 TIMES }

activities.bonus_note: Robot cleaning loop. Accept any valid loop using the robot's allowed actions.

activities.title: Yes/No Conditionals

challenge.label: Build Your Own Conditional
items: **Part A A1:** IF you put in $2 THEN give snack OTHERWISE return money
**Part A A2:** IF it's 8:00 AM THEN go inside OTHERWISE keep playing
**Part A A3:** IF you have a library card THEN borrow the book OTHERWISE sign up at the desk
**Part B:** Diamond "Is temperature below 15°C?" → Yes: "Wear a jacket" / No: "Leave it at home"

activities.bonus_note: Real-world conditional hunt. Accept any 4 valid IF-THEN-OTHERWISE conditionals from real life.

activities.title: Nested Conditionals

challenge.label: Build a Nested Conditional
items: **Part A A1:** IF it's a school day THEN { IF raining THEN wait in cafeteria OTHERWISE go outside } OTHERWISE stay home
**Part A A2:** IF book is overdue THEN pay a fine OTHERWISE { IF book is damaged THEN pay damage fee OTHERWISE no charge }

**Part B Bug:** Inner OTHERWISE says "walk the dog outside" — but the outer OTHERWISE already means the weather isn't good. Contradiction. **Fix:** change the inner OTHERWISE to "give the dog a treat."

activities.bonus_note: Open-ended decision tree. Accept any 2-question tree that produces 3 distinct outcomes.

activities.title: Debug the Robot

basic.label: Find the Bug
items: **Program 1 (Tea):** SEQUENCE BUG. "Drink the tea" is out of order — should be LAST.
**Program 2 (Walking Dog):** MISSING-STEP BUG. No step to put leash on dog. Add "Put leash on the dog" between steps 1 and 2.
**Program 3 (Alarm):** WRONG-VALUE BUG. 7:00 PM should be 7:00 AM.

challenge.label: Two-Bug Hunt
items: **Part A (Juice):** Bug 1 — Step 3 is a duplicate. Delete it. Bug 2 — "Wash the glass" should be AFTER the guest finishes drinking.
**Part B (Watering loop):** Loop runs 4 times but there are 5 plants. **Fix:** change 4 to 5. Bonus insight if kid also notices the loop doesn't move to the next plant.

activities.bonus_note: Open-ended "Be the Bug." Accept any 5-step instruction set with a deliberate, identifiable bug.

items: **Part B:** The INNER loop runs more times. For every ONE pass of the outer loop, the inner loop runs through every pair. Bubble sort's power (and cost) comes from nested loops.

challenge.label: Sort [8, 3, 5, 1, 4] Both Ways
items: **Selection Sort:** After Sel 1: [1, 3, 5, 8, 4]. After Sel 2: [1, 3, 5, 8, 4]. After Sel 3: [1, 3, 4, 8, 5]. After Sel 4: [1, 3, 4, 5, 8].
**Part B:** Open-ended discussion. Both are valid. Selection sort has fewer swaps; bubble sort can early-exit if list is nearly sorted.
**A1:** Fibonacci (add previous two). Next: **13**. Type: Rule-based.
**A2:** Differences decrease by 1 (−10, −9, −8, −7, then −6). Next: **60**. Type: Rule-based.
**A3:** Days of the week. Next: **S** (Sunday). Type: Repeating (or Rule-based — both accepted).

basic.label: Grid 1 (one valid path — others accepted)
basic.content: **MOVE → TURN RIGHT → MOVE → MOVE → TURN LEFT → MOVE → MOVE → MOVE → TURN LEFT → MOVE → MOVE** = **11 commands**

items: **Part A — Grid 2 with loops:** Without loops: 10 commands. With loops: TURN RIGHT → REPEAT MOVE 4 TIMES → TURN LEFT → REPEAT MOVE 4 TIMES = **4 commands**.

**Part B — Grid 3 debug:** Bug — step 4 walks Bolt into the wall at (1,1). **Fix:** TURN RIGHT before step 4. Corrected: MOVE → MOVE → MOVE → TURN RIGHT → MOVE → MOVE → TURN LEFT → MOVE = **8 commands**.

activities.title: Design Your Own Algorithm

basic.content: Open-ended. Accept any 5-step algorithm following the design process (pick problem → list steps → look for patterns → write → test).

challenge.content: Open-ended. Must use at least one **loop** AND one **conditional**.

activities.bonus_note: Open-ended (teach someone). The point is that the kid actually taught someone.
